title: Human Explanation — plain-language compiler errors with per-language hints
motto: Collect every error; explain in human terms; hint per language and retro platform.

patterns.explanation: The compiler finished reading a statement but did not find the semicolon that ends it.

hints.c: Add `;` at the end of this statement. In C, every expression statement and declaration ends with `;`.
hints.cxx: Add `;` after the statement. Do not confuse struct/class closing `}` with statement terminators.
hints.java: Java statements end with `;`. Check that this line is not inside a class body where a brace was forgotten.
hints.pascal: Pascal uses `;` between statements inside a `begin…end` block. A missing `;` before `else` is a common mistake.
hints.vb: Classic VB (VB6/VBA) rarely uses `;` — this error may mean you are in a C-style module or the wrong language profile.
hints.basic: Line-oriented BASIC does not use `;` terminators. Switch to the correct dialect profile (AMOS, Blitz, QBasic).
hints.asm: Do not blindly add `;` on label lines. M68k/68000 assemblers use `;` for comments, not statement ends.
hints.m68k: 68000 assembly: operand lines end with newline; labels have no trailing `;`. Semicolons start comments only.
hints.amiga: Amiga C (vbcc, GCC m68k-amigaos, SAS/C) uses normal `;` terminators. For Kickstart 1.x targets prefer `/* */` over `//` comments.
hints.amiga_68000: 68000 on Amiga: C needs `;`; assembly labels must not get an auto-appended `;`. Check you selected C vs asm profile.
hints.python: Python does not use `;` to end statements (except rarely on one line). This message often means the wrong language was selected.
hints.javascript: ASI (automatic semicolon insertion) may hide issues — add `;` explicitly if the linter/compiler asks for it.
hints.fortran: Fortran uses `;` only in free-form (F90+). Fixed-form uses column rules — verify free vs fixed profile.

patterns.explanation: A block header (def, class, if, for, etc.) was written without the colon that opens the indented block.

hints.python: Add `:` at the end of the `def`, `class`, `if`, `elif`, `else`, `for`, `while`, `try`, `except`, `with`, `match`, or `case` line.
hints.c: C does not use trailing `:`. This diagnostic usually means Python content was compiled as C — check language detection.
hints.amiga: AMOS/Blitz Basic use different block syntax — not Python colons. Select `amos` or `blitzbasic` profile.

match: was not declared in this scope
patterns.explanation: The compiler saw a name it does not know — no variable, function, type, or macro by that name is visible here.

hints.c: Add a declaration, `#include` the header, or forward-declare the function. Check spelling and scope.
hints.cxx: Add `#include`, a `using` declaration, namespace qualifier, or class member declaration.
hints.java: Declare the variable, import the class (`import pkg.Class;`), or fix the package name.
hints.pascal: Add the variable to `var` or the type to `type` section. Units must be in the `uses` clause.
hints.vb: Use `Dim` / `Public` / `Private` before first use. Check `Option Explicit` is not flagging typos.
hints.basic: Assign with `LET` or `DIM` arrays first. AMOS: declare with `:` inline or setup in initialization.
hints.asm: Labels must be defined before branch, or use `.global`/`.extern`. Check case sensitivity of the assembler.
hints.m68k: 68000: equates need `EQU`/`=` in assembler; in C, declare `extern` for asm symbols.
hints.amiga: Amiga SDK: include `proto/`, `exec/`, or `libraries/` headers. Link `-lamiga` for system calls.
hints.python: Define the name before use, or fix the import (`from module import name`).

patterns.explanation: A function was called without a prior declaration. Older C compilers assumed `int`; modern compilers reject this.

hints.c: Add a function prototype before use, or `#include` the header that declares it.
hints.amiga: AmigaOS headers in `include/proto/` declare most system calls — include the correct proto header.

patterns.explanation: Compilation succeeded for this file, but the linker cannot find the implementation of a function or global.

hints.c: Add the missing `.c` file to the build, or pass `-llibrary` to the linker.
hints.cxx: Ensure all translation units are linked. C++ needs `extern "C"` for C libraries sometimes.
hints.amiga: Link Amiga libraries: `-lamiga`, `-ldebug`, or add `.o` from NDK examples. Check vlink/GCC spec files.

patterns.explanation: The compiler hit tokens it cannot parse — often a missing bracket, quote, operator, or keyword in the wrong place.

hints.c: Check matching `()`, `{}`, `[]`. Look one line above — errors often report on the line after the real mistake.
hints.python: Check indentation (4 spaces), matching quotes, and parentheses. Mixed tabs/spaces cause IndentationError.
hints.pascal: Every `begin` needs `end`. `:=` is assignment; `=` is comparison in some dialects.
hints.vb: Line continuations use `_` at end of line. Block `If` needs `End If`.
hints.basic: AMOS: commands are line-based; string quotes are double. Blitz: types must be declared.
hints.asm: Check instruction operands match the ISA. M68k: address modes and size suffixes (`.w`, `.l`).
hints.amiga: Cross-check Amiga BASIC vs C profile. Hardware registers need `volatile` and correct include paths.

patterns.explanation: Python requires consistent indentation to define blocks. Mixed tabs and spaces or wrong depth breaks parsing.

hints.python: Use 4 spaces per level — no tabs. Align `except`/`elif` with matching `try`/`if`.
hints.c: C ignores indentation — if you see this, the file may be detected as Python by mistake.

patterns.explanation: A value is being used where the compiler expected a different type.

hints.c: Cast explicitly `(type)value` or fix the variable type. Check pointer vs value.
hints.cxx: Use `static_cast` / `reinterpret_cast` deliberately. Check template parameter types.
hints.java: Autoboxing may fail — use `.intValue()` or fix generic bounds.
hints.amiga: Amiga `ULONG`/`LONG` vs `unsigned long` — match NDK typedefs from `exec/types.h`.

patterns.explanation: Comparing to `None` with `==` works but `is None` is the idiomatic identity test in Python.

hints.python: Use `is None` / `is not None` for singleton checks. Use `==` only when `__eq__` semantics matter.

patterns.explanation: A brace-delimited block is not closed or opened correctly.

hints.c: Count `{` and `}`. Macro expansions can confuse line numbers — run `gcc -E` to preprocess if stuck.
hints.java: Each `{` in a class or method needs a matching `}`.
hints.pascal: Use `begin` / `end` instead of braces — verify dialect.
hints.amiga: Amiga C follows K&R/ANSI brace rules; SAS/C may warn on `{` after `if` on same line.

fallback.explanation: The compiler reported a problem at this location. Read the raw message for the exact token or symbol involved.

hints.c: Fix the line cited; then recompile. Check the first error first — later errors are often cascades.
hints.python: Run `python3 -m py_compile file.py` for a focused syntax check.
hints.amiga: Confirm m68k-amigaos toolchain, NDK includes, and that asm vs C profile matches the source.
hints.generic: Compare against a minimal working example in the same language profile.

notes.amiga: Semicolon autocorrect is allowed for C/C++ on Amiga only when lang is c/cxx and line is not asm-style.
notes.amiga_68000: Assembly profile: never append semicolons — 68000 uses ; for comments.
notes.retro_global: Confidence 1.0 fixes are verified by re-compile; rejected fixes stay in Human Explanation as hints only.

libretro_installed.note: Pre-installed cores; Amiga uses libretro_live/puae not in installed set
